channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Israel is building a buffer zone inside Lebanon
item.description: Israel is creating a large buffer zone in southern Lebanon for a prolonged military occupation, with low expectations that direct talks with Lebanon will lead to quick action on disarming Hezbollah.
item.content:encoded: Israel is creating a large buffer zone in southern Lebanon for a prolonged military occupation, with low expectations that direct talks with Lebanon will lead to quick action on disarming Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Veteran diplomat offers insights into the war in Iran — and thoughts on what's next
item.description: The war entered a new phase when President Trump began a U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Aaron David Miller of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace explains what this means.
item.content:encoded: The war entered a new phase when President Trump began a U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Aaron David Miller of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace explains what this means.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: After losing loved ones, an Israeli and a Palestinian work together for Middle East peace
item.description: An Israeli whose parents were killed on Oct. 7, 2023, and a Palestinian whose brother died from injuries in Israeli custody say they've become like brothers. Their new book is The Future Is Peace: A Shared Journey Across the Holy Land.
item.content:encoded:
An Israeli whose parents were killed on Oct. 7, 2023, and a Palestinian whose brother died from injuries in Israeli custody say they've become like brothers. Their new book is The Future Is Peace: A Shared Journey Across the Holy Land.

(Image credit: Maya Levin for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Fuel protests have Ireland's government facing possible no-confidence vote
item.description: The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.
item.content:encoded:
The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.

(Image credit: Peter Morrison)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Eric Swalwell will resign from Congress. And, Trump feuds with Pope Leo over Iran War
item.description: Eric Swalwell is resigning from Congress after multiple women accused him of sexual assault and misconduct. And, Trump is feuding with Pope Leo, calling him weak on crime for opposing the war.
item.content:encoded:
Eric Swalwell is resigning from Congress after multiple women accused him of sexual assault and misconduct. And, Trump is feuding with Pope Leo, calling him weak on crime for opposing the war.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to start peace negotiations after rare talks in D.C.
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the talks were meant to lay out a framework for lasting peace and to bring an end to Hezbollah's influence in the region.
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the talks were meant to lay out a framework for lasting peace and to bring an end to Hezbollah's influence in the region.

(Image credit: Kevin Lamarque)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Ukraine hopes for more cooperation after Hungary elects new prime minister
item.description: The incoming prime minister of Hungary is no fan of Ukraine, but he says he wants to work with the European Union, raising hopes he'll lift a Hungarian veto on a $100 billion EU loan to Kyiv.
item.content:encoded: The incoming prime minister of Hungary is no fan of Ukraine, but he says he wants to work with the European Union, raising hopes he'll lift a Hungarian veto on a $100 billion EU loan to Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: 'Hanging by a thread': Fertilizer shortage in India deepens due to ongoing Iran war
item.description: With shipments through the Strait of Hormuz stalled after the Iran war began, fertilizer shortages are deepening across India.
item.content:encoded: With shipments through the Strait of Hormuz stalled after the Iran war began, fertilizer shortages are deepening across India.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: U.K. inquiry lays bare 'catastrophic' missed chances before stabbings at dance class
item.description: An inquiry found that a mass killing by a British teenager in 2024 at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class could have been prevented if his parents and state agencies had acted on his violence fixation.
item.content:encoded:
An inquiry found that a mass killing by a British teenager in 2024 at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class could have been prevented if his parents and state agencies had acted on his violence fixation.

(Image credit: Scott Heppell)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: After years of speculation, a reporter claims to have uncovered the founder of Bitcoin
item.description: After years of speculation, New York Times reporter John Carreyrou explains why he thinks he identified the true founder of Bitcoin.
item.content:encoded: After years of speculation, New York Times reporter John Carreyrou explains why he thinks he identified the true founder of Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly
